semantic.role: Configuration defining scanning patterns for Ruby code analysis
responsibilities: Defining import detection patterns (require, require_relative, load)
Defining export detection patterns (class, module, function)
Maintenance of Ruby code analysis rules
outOfScope: Actual Ruby source code analysis logic
Business rules of the ArchSpine mirror system

English.purpose: To provide a machine-readable specification for how ArchSpine should parse Ruby source files to understand dependencies and symbols.
English.context_and_audience: This document is intended for developers configuring the ArchSpine code analysis engine, specifically for Ruby projects. It defines the pattern rules for imports, exports, and usages that govern how the system extracts structural metadata from Ruby code.
key_takeaways: The file uses YAML to define pattern rules for imports, exports, and usages.
Each pattern has an id, a pattern string, and optionally a kind field for exports.
Imports are matched via require, require_relative, and load statements.
Exports include classes, modules, and functions (def).
Usages cover method calls and generic function calls.
Maintenance involves updating patterns to support Ruby language changes or project-specific conventions.
